title: A New Age of War Is Declared
description: On pages 7–9, this part follows Unknown person #7, King Harald, Usopp, Unknown person #8, Unknown person #9 as Elbaf’s warriors, Jarul, and the younger giants clash over the future of the island while dark forces twist pride and tradition into violence.
scene: Elbaf’s warriors, Jarul, and the younger giants clash over the future of the island while dark forces twist pride and tradition into violence.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I was born on Samlwanai Island in the South Blue.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: One thing led to another, and I ended up on these seas.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: You went over the blood-soaked serpent?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Are you talking about the Red line?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Well, yeah I did... but I can't go back now.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Come to Elbaf! We're not as savage as you think!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Yeah, maybe I judged you too harshly.

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: King Harald has really changed.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's kind of creepy... hopefully it's just a phase.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Elbaf, 105 years ago

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Up until now, we've committed countless sins...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: ...and it's time we alone for them!

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: What's wrong with you, Harald?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As King, I intend to frequently travel overseas...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I hope to extend a hand of reconciliation to our neighbors and learn from their cultures.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Our Land Can Become More prosperous...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: If we learn from the world!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Dorry, Brogy, and the others will never stop running wild!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Waha Ha!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I GO WAY BACK WITH THOSE TWO! THEY HAVE ALWAYS HAD AN UNUSUAL DISPOSITION.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They've never failed to treat humans fairly and honorably.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: That's true!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We can still hold on to our Warrior ways, right?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We may have to reconsider some traditions if other countries feel threatened.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Zaba BA BA! What a change!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: BojA JA! Seems there's a tumultuous journey ahead!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Harald is... no, Elbaf is...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Blessed.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Say we exchange diplomats with other nations...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: We could build a deeper cultural understanding.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I see... so it's like a fancy hostage deal, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: That's not it at all!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Otherwise, international cooperation is impossible.

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Elbaf, 81 years ago

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You finally had the baby?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Eastern fishing village

dialogue.speaker: King Harald
dialogue.text: IDA, Harald!! Congratulations!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Thank you, IDA and I-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Unfortunately, we can't recognize your marriage!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Only pure blood can be permitted within the Royal Family!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: DAMN IT... FORGIVE ME, IDA!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: All these issues boil down to culture. Our feelings alone won't change things.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You're always welcome in our village, IDA!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Isn't that great, hajurdin?! At least you can stay near your papa!:)

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: GAAAA!!

summary: This part advances the The Giants Turn on Their Legend through 45 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on the immediate conflict, character reactions, and the larger Elbaf mystery.
